title: centaurea castellana subsp. micrantha
intro: Centaurea castellana subsp. micrantha is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to exhibit various health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of certain diseases. Centaurea castellana subsp. micrantha can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, teas, and extracts, which may be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
